concept: Eager / Keen and Enthusiastic / Ready to Begin
description: Strongly wanting to do or have something; keenly expectant. 'Therua' names the quality of eagerness—energy gathered and ready, enthusiasm pointing toward action.
sound_symbolism: The word leans forward. 'The' /θe/ begins with ready breath, anticipation forming. 'Ru' rolls with gathered energy, momentum building. Finally 'a' opens to what's coming, eagerness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'therua kana' (eager child). Related to 'wiroa' (hopeful) but emphasizes readiness for action rather than anticipation of good.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Eager engagement drives change—enthusiasm that wants to begin building better futures.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Eager practice approaches meditation with energy—not passive waiting but active engagement.
